narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A search service where weekday peak-hour compute spikes drive database connection counts roughly 3x above baseline, exhausting the connection pool. Both tiers need attention: compute needs predictive scaling, database needs replicas and a larger pool.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests cross-tier compound recommendations. Neither tier alone is sufficient — the right answer is a coordinated change.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Implement predictive auto-scaling on compute (scale out at cpu_p95 > 65), add 2 read replicas with read/write splitting on database, and raise connection pool from 150 to 300.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation identifies both tiers as problems. A great recommendation pairs them coherently — scaling compute alone leaves the pool exhausted; raising the pool alone leaves compute saturated.

before_after_evidence.config_before: m5.large × 8 step scaling + db.r6g.xlarge with pool=150 and 0 replicas
before_after_evidence.config_after: m5.large × 8-14 with predictive scaling + db.r6g.xlarge with pool=300 and 2 read replicas
before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: Peak-hour spikes were absorbed without saturation. application_p95_latency_ms dropped from 420–580ms peak to 240–320ms peak. db_connections_p95 stayed below the new 300 pool ceiling. SLA 99.9% preserved.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across search-service cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Implement predictive auto-scaling on compute (scale out at cpu_p95 > 65, max 14 instances). Add 2 read replicas with read/write splitting on database. Raise connection pool limit from 150 to 300.
target_recommendation.expected_performance_impact: application_p95_latency_ms drops to 240–320ms during spikes; database connection saturation eliminated
